title: Decadent Indian Tomato Samosas
description: Fermented, crisp air‑fried samosas filled with a tangy tomato juice mixture, low‑sodium and indulgent.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together flour, yeast, and salt.
Add warm water and 2 tbsp olive oil, and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Turn dough onto a floured surface, knead for 6-8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let it rise in a warm spot for about 30 minutes or until doubled in size.
Meanwhile, whisk together tomato juice, onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, and 1 tbsp olive oil in a saucepan.
Cook the mixture over medium heat for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the liquid reduces slightly.
Remove from heat, stir in chopped cilantro, and let the filling cool to room temperature.
Punch down the risen dough, divide into 8 equal portions, and roll each into a thin disc.
Cut each disc in half, then fold into triangles, sealing the edges with a pinch of water and a dab of oil to keep them crisp.
Preheat an air fryer to 200°C and lightly spray the basket with oil.
Place the samosas in a single layer in the basket, air‑fry for 12-15 minutes, turning halfway, until golden and crisp.
Serve warm with a side of low-sodium chutney or plain yogurt.
